name: Folgueroles - Els Munts - Pantà de Sau - Savassona - Tavèrnoles Loop
name_original: Folgueroles, Salt de la Minyona, Pantano de Sau - circular
description: This difficult 21.5-mile mountain biking loop offers technical challenges and stunning views of the Sau reservoir and Guilleries-Savassona.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Folgueroles offers diverse terrain, situated where the gentle plains of Vic meet the rugged Guilleries-Savassona Natural Area. This creates a varied landscape with cultivated fields, small blue marl hills, and dense European and Mediterranean forests. The region features ravines and bare rock cliffs, providing a range of challenges for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Folgueroles The most popular mountain bike trail is Folgueroles - Els Munts - Pantà de Sau - Savassona - Tavèrnoles Loop, a 21.5 miles (34.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 19 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant climbs and descents, traversing varied landscapes including views of the Sau reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is View of the Pyrenees – Viladrau Town Square loop from Folgueroles, a moderate 29.2 miles (47.0 km) path. This route offers expansive views of the Pyrenees and passes through forested areas. Local mountain bikers also love the Summit – Gorg dels Llitons loop from Folgueroles, a 12.8 miles (20.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 55 minutes. Mountain biking around Folgueroles is defined by a blend of gentle plains, rugged forested areas, ravines, and hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Folgueroles are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from several reviews. More than 290 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Folgueroles's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Folgueroles?
A: Folgueroles offers a diverse landscape for mountain biking, situated where the gentle plains of Vic meet the rugged Guilleries-Savassona Natural Area. You'll encounter cultivated dryland fields, small hills of blue marl, and dense European and Mediterranean forests with ravines and bare rock cliffs. This variety provides routes ranging from relaxed rides on the plains to technical trails in more mountainous, wooded areas.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Folgueroles?
A: Yes, Folgueroles has options for various skill levels. While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are 3 easy trails available. These are suitable for those with less experience or families looking for a more relaxed ride. You can find more details on komoot to filter by difficulty.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural landmarks I can see while mountain biking in Folgueroles?
A: The region offers several scenic spots. You can enjoy expansive views of the Pyrenees on routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19184216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Pyrenees – Viladrau Town Square loop from Folgueroles&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3017238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Sant Sebastià&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5074387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gurb Cross&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5402851" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Matagalls from GR-2&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4039178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt del Molí Bernat&lt;/a&gt; waterfall is also a beautiful natural feature to discover.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Folgueroles?
A: Many of the mountain bike trails in Folgueroles are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958740994/folgueroles-els-munts-panta-de-sau-savassona-tavernoles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Folgueroles - Els Munts - Pantà de Sau - Savassona - Tavèrnoles Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40360442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit – Gorg dels Llitons loop from Folgueroles&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Folgueroles?
A: Folgueroles experiences a Mediterranean mountain climate with continental influences, meaning cold winters and generally cool summers. This makes it suitable for outdoor activities across various seasons. Spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, avoiding the colder winter months and the peak heat of summer, though specific conditions can vary.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Folgueroles?
A: There are over 25 mountain bike trails around Folgueroles listed on komoot, offering a wide range of distances and difficulties. This includes 3 easy, 10 moderate, and 14 difficult routes, ensuring options for all skill levels.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Folgueroles?
A: The mountain bike trails in Folgueroles are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the blend of gentle plains and rugged forested areas, and the challenging ascents and descents that cater to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites accessible by mountain bike in Folgueroles?
A: Yes, Folgueroles has a rich cultural heritage. The town itself features centenary stone houses. You can explore literary routes dedicated to the renowned Catalan poet Mossèn Cinto Verdaguer, who was born here. Some mountain bike routes, like the 'Ruta Verdagueriana amb tocs Brossians,' integrate cultural exploration with physical activity. Additionally, ancient quarries and sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedra de les Bruixes Rock Engravings&lt;/a&gt; offer glimpses into the region's history.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Folgueroles is known for its more rugged terrain, especially within the Guilleries-Savassona Natural Area. There are 14 difficult routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32097370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Creu de la Miranda – Sant Quirze de Muntanyola loop from Folgueroles&lt;/a&gt;, which require good physical condition and advanced riding skills, potentially involving technical sections and significant climbs.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay in Folgueroles after my ride?
A: Yes, Folgueroles is a charming village with amenities. You can find restaurants and cafes to refuel after your ride. For longer stays, there are accommodation options available in and around the village, allowing you to fully experience the area's natural beauty and cultural offerings.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that offer views of the Sau reservoir?
A: Yes, several routes provide stunning views of the Sau reservoir. The challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958740994/folgueroles-els-munts-panta-de-sau-savassona-tavernoles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Folgueroles - Els Munts - Pantà de Sau - Savassona - Tavèrnoles Loop&lt;/a&gt; is one such trail that traverses varied landscapes including impressive vistas of the reservoir.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on mountain bike trails in Folgueroles?
A: The elevation gain varies significantly depending on the trail's difficulty and length. For instance, a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40360442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit – Gorg dels Llitons loop from Folgueroles&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of around 395 meters, while more difficult and longer routes can feature climbs exceeding 700 or 800 meters, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19184216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Pyrenees – Viladrau Town Square loop from Folgueroles&lt;/a&gt; with over 850 meters of ascent.
tips: Beautiful viewpoint over the Guilleries. From the Sau reservoir to the Montseny and the Plana de Vic. Must visit
Beautiful views of the Sau reservoir and the Cingles de Tavertet (Riscos de Tavertet) from this viewpoint located right next to the pier.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: This challenging mountain bike loop typically takes around 3 hours and 20 minutes to complete. However, given its difficult rating and technical sections, allow for more time depending on your skill level and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this loop is considered difficult and is designed for experienced mountain biking enthusiasts. It features technical sections, steep descents, and physically demanding parts. Some sections may even require dismounting and walking your bike, especially on descents from the cliffs and leaving the Savassona plain.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular views and passes several notable features. You'll encounter the Salto de la Miñona viewpoint, and enjoy breathtaking views of Sau Reservoir and Cingles de Tavertet. The trail also takes you through the magical Sant Feliuet de Savassona Forest and past the Iberian and medieval settlement of Savassona.

faqs.question: Where can I start this mountain bike loop and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: Folgueroles is a common starting point for this loop, and parking is generally available there. Alternatively, you can also begin the route from Tavèrnoles or Vilanova de Sau, which also offer access points to the trail.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and demanding, characteristic of a difficult mountain bike route. Expect a mix of technical singletrack and various types of forest paths. There are steep ascents and descents, and some sections may be rocky or uneven, requiring good bike handling skills.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder, potentially wet conditions, making the technical sections more challenging.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the Espai Natural de les Guilleries-Savassona, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially given the technical nature of the trail, potential encounters with wildlife, and shared sections with hikers. The challenging terrain might also be difficult for some dogs.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Espai Natural de les Guilleries-Savassona?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to access this mountain bike loop or the Espai Natural de les Guilleries-Savassona. However, always respect local regulations and private property signage.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other trails. Notably, it covers significant portions of the Folgueroles-Salt de la Minyona-Tavèrnoles and Folgueroles - Els Munts - Pantà de Sau - Savassona - Tavèrnoles - Folgueroles routes. You'll also find sections that overlap with the Camí Oliba and Ruta del Ter.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, and a first-aid kit. A helmet is essential, and protective gear like gloves and knee pads are highly recommended. A GPS device or a fully charged phone with the Komoot app is crucial for navigation, as the route is part of the BTT Vall de Sau-Collsacabra center (route 11) but a GPS is recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any specific safety considerations for this challenging route?
faqs.answer: Yes, due to its technical nature and steep sections, exercise caution, especially on descents. Be aware that some areas, particularly from the cliffs in Vilanova de Sau and leaving the Savassona plain, are also frequented by hikers and have limited visibility. Always maintain control, be prepared to dismount, and yield to hikers.

waypoints.name: View of Sau Reservoir and Cingles de Tavertet
